Welcome
PGS at FNLGHTC
Your guide to understanding our strategy.
What is the PGS?
The Performance Governance System (PGS) is the framework used by Philippine government hospitals to translate strategy into action. It is built around a Strategy Map — a one-page picture of where we are going and how we plan to get there. The PGS is implemented in four stages: Initiation, Compliance, Proficiency, and Institutionalization.
Where are we now?
FNLGHTC is currently at Stage 2 — Compliance. The key outcome of this stage is showing evidence of strategy execution. This means every employee must understand their role and how their work connects to the strategy. Our next milestone is the Gold Trailblazer Award in 2026.
How to use this tool
This tool acts like your guide to our PGS Journey. Explore our strategy map layer by layer, learn the PGS stages and what each one means, and best of all, test yourself with the Study Mode. Lastly, you can also generate your contribution statement too!
Strategy Map 2028
Our Strategic Direction
Tap each layer to explore. The map reads from the bottom up — what enables us, to what we deliver.
Strategic Position
FNLGHTC is positioning itself to become a 200-bed Level 3 hospital with 5 accredited residency training programs, Basic Comprehensive Capability in neonatal care, and a Cancer Control Unit serving the province of Apayao and the coastal towns of Cagayan by 2028
Vision

Far North Luzon General Hospital and Training Center as a premier multispecialty hospital setting the standard of excellence in patient care, training, and research — committed to the holistic wellness of Apayao and nearby towns of Cagayan.

Mission

FNLGHTC affirms to serve the people of Apayao and Cagayan by:

• Delivering quality, people-centered, accessible, and affordable health services;

• Producing medical and allied healthcare professionals who are competent, compassionate, and service- oriented; and

• Providing policy-driven, socially relevant, and bioethical researches that are locally- and globally-recognized

Impact
What we deliver to our community
▼
Better Health Outcomes
Stronger Health Systems
Access to All Levels of Care
Core Strategies
What we will focus on
▼
Deliver specialty services in Basic Comprehensive Capabilities in Neonatal Care, and cancer control (screening, early detection, and management) strengthened by an interdisciplinary approach
Establish and strengthen 5 accredited residency training programs and explained training opportunities accross hospital divisions
Produce descriptive and analytical, clinical epidemiology, and operations management departmentalized research outputs overseen by PETRU and PHREB Level 1 accredited REC
Enhance health-seeking behavior by implementing a functional two-way referral system and fostering community engagement in the entire catchment area
Institutionalize an integrated safety program guided by national and international standards with online reporting overseen by functional safety committees
Support Strategies
Enabling conditions
▼
Establish a process-defined human resource management system for a Level 3 hospital with 200-bed staffing pattern
Implement an efficient Management Information System for Patient and Support Services responsive to regulatory requirements and current trends in Information Technology
Comply with infrastructure and equipment requirements for level 3, Neonatal Care Services, and Cancer Control Unit while laying the groundwork for a 250-bed APEX hospital
Enhance revenue generating capabilities through responsive PhilHealth packages and implementation of an integrated financial management system to ensure efficient utilization of funds

PGS Journey
The Four Stages of the PGS Governance Pathway
Tap a stage to learn more. Each stage builds on the previous one.
1
Initiation
Planning the strategy
▼
The organization creates a comprehensive plan outlining its goals, strategies, and roadmap. FNLGHTC completed this in 2024 — building the Strategy Map and earning the Gold Trailblazer Award.
2
Compliance We are here
Execution and cascading
▼
The focus is execution and cascading. Everyone must understand their role and how it connects to the strategy. The key outcome is showing evidence of strategy execution. FNLGHTC aims for another Gold Award in 2026.

What this means for you:
Organizational Alignment Strategy Cascading Scorecards Development Execution of Key Initiatives Feedback Loops Documentation and Reporting
3
Proficiency
Monitoring and refining
▼
The organization shifts to monitoring, evaluating, and refining the execution of the strategy. Performance data drives decision-making.
4
Institutionalization
Sustaining reforms and delivering results
▼
The highest stage — strategy governance is fully embedded in day-to-day operations, sustaining reforms and consistently delivering results.
The Nine Elements
PGS Elements
Eight governance mechanisms plus breakthrough results compose the nine elements of the PGS.

In implementing the Performance Governance System, organizations assure the implementation of eight (8) governance mechanisms to achieve breakthrough results to its stakeholders. These eight mechanisms and the breakthrough result compose the nine (9) elements of the PGS.

In order to reach the last stage of the PGS, organizations should be able to show a systemic and systematic approach in their delivery of performance with the commitment of all in actively pursuing the organizational priorities.

1
Basic Governance Documents
The organization's strategic position, a strategy map, a set of yearly deliverables (or roadmaps), and a governance scorecard
Strategy Design
2
PGS Core Team
Champions of the strategy within the different operational units
3
Cascading Framework
Aligning every unit and personnel to the strategy
4
Office for Strategy Management
A unit created purposively to oversee strategy execution
5
Strategic Performance Assessments
Creating a discipline of accountability with regular and effective strategy and operations reviews
6
Multi-sector Governance Council
Formalizing the participation of key external stakeholders to the strategy
Strategy Execution
7
Governance Culture
Creating a culture of good governance
8
Governance Sharing
Sharing the good governance advocacy internally and externally
Strategy Sustainability
9
Validated Breakthrough Results
Attained strategic goals with impact to stakeholders
Breakthrough Results

How Do I Contribute?
Fill out the form below to align your unit's work with the FNLGHTC Strategy Map 2028.
Why cascade?

At the Compliance Stage, every unit must show how its daily work contributes to the organizational strategy. Cascading your goals down from the Strategy Map makes your contribution visible — and ensures you are working on the right things.
